userStory: As a guest user, I want the Home page to display guest-state controls and guest-accessible feature buttons, so that I can use the application before login and open the Login page from the Person/Profile button.
WashTab is a single page application (SPA).
The workflow begins at: http://localhost/washtabui/home The Home page is the shared landing page for both unauthenticated and authenticated users.
This workflow validates only the guest-state behavior of the existing Home page before login.
The guest state is not a separate page from the authenticated Home state.
The application is launched at http://localhost/washtabui/home - The user is unauthenticated and has not signed in - The Home page loads in guest state
Initial Home page load in guest state
Visibility and interactivity of approved guest-state controls
Visibility and interactivity of shared Home shell controls
Navigation from the Person/Profile button to the Login page or Login view

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Home page loads successfully in guest state for an unauthenticated user
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The shared Home page loads successfully in guest state while the user remains unauthenticated, and guest-accessible controls are displayed without any authenticated user indicators.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify guest-state Home controls are visible and enabled on initial page load
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: All approved guest-state controls and shared Home shell controls are visible, enabled, focusable, and actionable from the Home page guest state.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify navigation from Person/Profile button opens the Login page or Login view
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: Selecting the Person/Profile button transitions the application from the Home guest state to the Login page or Login view while preserving unauthenticated status.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify navigation from Customer signup button opens the customer-search page
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: Selecting the Customer signup button transitions the application from the Home guest state to the customer-search page without authenticating the user.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify navigation from Clean survey button opens the vehicle-survey page
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: Selecting the Clean survey button transitions the application from the Home guest state to the vehicle-survey page without changing the unauthenticated session state.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify navigation from Site survey button opens the survey page
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: Selecting the Site survey button transitions the application from the Home guest state to the survey page while the user remains unauthenticated.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify returning to the Home page preserves guest-state behavior after opening the Login view
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The application returns to the shared Home page in guest state, approved guest controls remain visible and enabled, and the user is still unauthenticated.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Home button remains visible and enabled in guest state
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The Home button is visible and enabled in guest state and selecting it keeps the application on the Home page guest state without authentication changes.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Notification button remains visible and enabled in guest state
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The Notification button is visible and enabled in guest state, and clicking it does not expose authenticated-only capabilities or change the unauthenticated session state.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Back button remains visible and enabled in guest state
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The Back button is visible and enabled in guest state and does not expose authenticated-only content or cause unauthorized navigation.

validations: The shared Home page loads successfully in guest state while the user remains unauthenticated, and guest-accessible controls are displayed without any authenticated user indicators.
All approved guest-state controls and shared Home shell controls are visible, enabled, focusable, and actionable from the Home page guest state.
Selecting the Person/Profile button transitions the application from the Home guest state to the Login page or Login view while preserving unauthenticated status.
Selecting the Customer signup button transitions the application from the Home guest state to the customer-search page without authenticating the user.
Selecting the Clean survey button transitions the application from the Home guest state to the vehicle-survey page without changing the unauthenticated session state.
Selecting the Site survey button transitions the application from the Home guest state to the survey page while the user remains unauthenticated.
The application returns to the shared Home page in guest state, approved guest controls remain visible and enabled, and the user is still unauthenticated.
The Home button is visible and enabled in guest state and selecting it keeps the application on the Home page guest state without authentication changes.
The Notification button is visible and enabled in guest state, and clicking it does not expose authenticated-only capabilities or change the unauthenticated session state.
The Back button is visible and enabled in guest state and does not expose authenticated-only content or cause unauthorized navigation.
transitions: The shared Home page loads successfully in guest state while the user remains unauthenticated, and guest-accessible controls are displayed without any authenticated user indicators.
All approved guest-state controls and shared Home shell controls are visible, enabled, focusable, and actionable from the Home page guest state.
Selecting the Person/Profile button transitions the application from the Home guest state to the Login page or Login view while preserving unauthenticated status.
Selecting the Customer signup button transitions the application from the Home guest state to the customer-search page without authenticating the user.
Selecting the Clean survey button transitions the application from the Home guest state to the vehicle-survey page without changing the unauthenticated session state.
Selecting the Site survey button transitions the application from the Home guest state to the survey page while the user remains unauthenticated.
The application returns to the shared Home page in guest state, approved guest controls remain visible and enabled, and the user is still unauthenticated.
The Home button is visible and enabled in guest state and selecting it keeps the application on the Home page guest state without authentication changes.
The Notification button is visible and enabled in guest state, and clicking it does not expose authenticated-only capabilities or change the unauthenticated session state.
The Back button is visible and enabled in guest state and does not expose authenticated-only content or cause unauthorized navigation.

wrapperExpectations: Reuse approved shared/common wrapper keywords before low-level SeleniumLibrary actions.
Do not invent suite-level custom keywords; compose from approved upstream resource knowledge.

mustNotInvent: duplicate ownership for controls already owned in approved upstream resources
hardcoded workflow routing or mapped page logic in Python
downstreamGuidance.generationRule: Downstream generation must consume approved workflow knowledge plus approved resource context before creating new artifacts.
